docs.title: iLab Maker Journal
docs.text:
Welcome to my personal activity log as a Maker Mentor and STEM Mentor at iLab.

This journal documents my mentoring sessions, the maker projects I've guided, and the learnings that come out of each experience.

docs.title: What This Journal Covers
docs.text:
• Mentoring Sessions — one-on-one and group sessions with makers and students
  • Maker Projects Guided — projects I've helped design, prototype, or debug
  • Workshops & Activities — any events, demos, or hands-on activities I facilitated
  • Reflections — what worked, what didn't, and what's next

docs.title: Journal Structure
docs.text:
Each log entry follows this format:

## Overview
What happened this session.

## Mentees / Participants
Who I worked with.

## Projects Guided
Projects discussed or built.

## Activities
What we did hands-on.

## Highlights
Key wins or breakthroughs.

## Challenges
Blockers or areas to improve.

## Next Steps
Follow-ups or upcoming plans.

docs.title: About the Mentor
docs.text:
I am a Maker Mentor / STEM Mentor at iLab, helping students and makers turn ideas into real projects through hands-on guidance and community learning.

How to navigate

Use the Journal tab above to browse all session logs, or use the search bar to find entries by topic, project, or date.

docs.title: Who I Am
docs.text: I am a Maker Mentor at iLab, passionate about hands-on learning, prototyping, and helping makers bring their ideas to life.

docs.title: What I Do at iLab
docs.text:
• Guide students and makers through hardware and software projects
  • Facilitate workshops and skill-building sessions
  • Support project planning, prototyping, and iteration
  • Build a culture of curiosity and making

docs.title: Why I Keep This Journal
docs.text:
This journal helps me:

  • Track my growth as a mentor over time
  • Document the projects and ideas coming out of iLab
  • Reflect on what's working in my mentoring approach
  • Share learnings with the broader maker community

Built with MkDocs Material

docs.title: Session Journal
docs.text: A chronological log of my mentoring sessions and maker project work at iLab.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Introductory STEM session for 15 students at iLab. Covered the basics of electricity and circuits, with hands-on LED and Arduino activities.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• What is electricity
  • How circuits work
  • LEDs — what they are and how to use them
  • Basic coding to blink an LED
  • Arduino UNO intro

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
• Slide walkthrough on electricity basics
  • Live Arduino + breadboard + LED demo
  • Students experimented with circuit connections and wrote blink code

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Students grasped electron flow quickly with a simple analogy
  • Seeing code control a physical LED was a big "wow" moment
  • Lots of questions and curiosity throughout
